Vytlacil2002 + HeckmanVytlacil2005: monotonicity/threshold-crossing equivalence and LIV/MTE derivative object

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: The entry keyed JiangSunHu2024PartialMonotonicityTest misidentifies the paper: Economics Letters 233, article 111400 is Jiang and Sun, 2023, not Jiang-Sun-Hu 2024.

novelty_flags.label: Related work / JiangSunHu2024PartialMonotonicityTest
novelty_flags.one_line: The named comparator does not exist as cited; the partial-instrument-monotonicity test is Jiang and Sun 2023/2024 publication metadata without Hu, though the multidimensional-instrument claim is otherwise on-axis.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The pointwise derivative-envelope relaxation being refuted is not tied to a specific published estimator, paper, or workflow that actually reports such locally pasted continuous-IV bounds.

novelty_flags.label: Proposal tier
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the proposal must compare against ChesherRosen2017 generalized IV sharp identified-set machinery and explain why the derivative-cone certificate is a regime-opening object rather than a model-specific convex-analysis specialization.

soundness_flags.label: Locked parameters / Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: With fixed envelope b, C_b is not a cone under positive scaling because c_- <= b is not homogeneous; the polar-cone gap Delta_b(P) is therefore not the right feasibility certificate without reformulating C_b as a convex set with a support-function separation.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed decomposition into positive-response, negative-response, and stay-put components is not mathematically specified: the component measures, signs, cell equations, and exact local inequalities are left as prose.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the fixed-b object C_b by a closed convex feasible set K_b and define Delta_b using support functions/separation for K_b; reserve cone language only for the homogeneous unbounded-variation generator if needed.

Add a published target for Conjecture 2: name a continuous-IV sensitivity, LIV, or MTE workflow whose implicit pointwise-pasting relaxation is being sharpened, or downgrade the strictness claim to an internal sanity separation.

Add a related-work paragraph on Chesher and Rosen (2017) and, if kept, Gunsilius-style continuous-IV/path-space bounds, spelling out why their generic sharp-set machinery does not already give the finite-threshold derivative certificate.
Rewrite Theorem 1 as explicit signed-measure inequalities for dg_y and dh_y, including all D=1 and D=0 subdistribution balance equations and the exact total-variation bounds.

recommended_next_step: Revise the convex-analytic object, fix the Jiang/Sun citation, and anchor the strict pointwise-vs-global separation to a named published continuous-IV workflow before resubmission.
